# Figure description: Схема_изопахит_Владимировской_свиты.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/plan_data/Схема_изопахит_Владимировской_свиты.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is an **isopach map** (схема изопахит) — a contour map showing the thickness variations of a specific stratigraphic unit across a region. It is drawn at a scale of **1:500,000** and uses isoline contours with colour-intensity shading to represent thickness zones.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The map covers a portion of **northern Kazakhstan** within the framework of the Central Kazakhstan Production Geological Association (Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение), Central Geological Prospecting Expedition. It depicts the thickness distribution of the **Vladimirskaya Suite (C₃vl)** — Upper Carboniferous continental red-bed deposits (верхнекаменноугольных континентальных красноцветных отложений). A **sheet layout diagram** (схема расположения листов) is shown in the lower right, indicating a multi-sheet grid with Roman numerals (I through XIV or similar), suggesting the map covers several 1:200,000 sheets including parts of the **Tenisskaya Depression (Тенисская впадина)**. A note in the lower right refers to the **Zapadno-Tenisskaya party report for 1984**.

---

## Key Content

**Isopach contours** delineate the thickness of the Vladimirskaya Suite across the mapped area. Key features include:

- **Thickness scale (Шкала интенсивности изопахит, в м):** The legend bar shows thickness intervals of approximately **0 – 200 – 400 – 600 – 800 – 1000+ metres**, represented by progressively deeper pink/red shading.
- **Thickest zones** (darkest pink/red shading, >800–1000 m) are concentrated in:
  - The **southern portion** of the map, corresponding to a depocenter likely within or near the Tenisskaya Depression.
  - A smaller **northern area** near the top of the map.
- **Intermediate thicknesses** (200–600 m, light pink shading) form broad aureoles around the depocenters, particularly across the central and southwestern portions of the map.
- **Zero or near-zero thickness** (unshaded/white areas with heavy black hatching) mark zones where the suite is **absent or eroded** — these correspond to structural highs or uplifted blocks where basement or older units crop out.
- **Dark hatched areas** (cross-hatching or diagonal hatching in black) represent:
  - **Outcrops of the Vladimirskaya Suite** at the present-day surface (выходы на дневную поверхность).
  - **Zones of correlation loss** (зоны потери корреляции отражённых волн) — areas where seismic reflectors become discontinuous, likely due to facies changes or structural complexity.
- **Dashed contours** in some areas indicate **inferred or interpolated isopachs** where data coverage is sparse.
- The **boundary of the Tenisskaya Depression** (граница Тенисской впадины) is shown as a distinct boundary line.
- **Seismically inferred contours** (узорчатые начертания предполагаемые по сейсмическим данным) are distinguished from drilled/mapped ones.

---

## Research Value

This isopach map directly quantifies the **preserved thickness and spatial distribution of the Upper Carboniferous Vladimirskaya (= Tersakanskaya) red-bed continental suite**, revealing a major **sedimentary depocenter** in the Tenisskaya Depression with thicknesses exceeding 1000 m — a critical indicator of the extent and depth of late Carboniferous basin subsidence. For mineral exploration
